title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy scalp

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb contains compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by itchiness. Additionally, stinging nettle's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to eliminate any underlying fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. By addressing these underlying issues, stinging nettle tinctures can provide relief from an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy and comfortable hair growth environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp, providing relief from conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.

Antioxidant: Nettle's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to skin issues on the scalp, such as dryness, flakiness, and itchiness.

Anti-allergic: Stinging nettle contains compounds that have been shown to reduce histamine release, a key factor in allergic reactions, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving itching and irritation caused by allergies or sensitivities.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Urtica dioica, such as histamine and serotonin receptors modulators, may help alleviate itchiness by suppressing the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, a class of phenolic compounds found in Urtica dioica, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and calm the skin and scalp, reducing itchiness and inflammation.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid glycoside, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may contribute to their potential in reducing scalp itchiness and promoting healthy skin and scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory and antipruritic compounds that help alleviate itchy scalp conditions.
Stems: Like the leaves, the stems also contain anti-inflammatory and antipruritic compounds, making them a popular choice for itchy scalp tinctures.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica contain a higher concentration of antipruritic compounds, which help to soothe and calm itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 100 grams of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems early in the morning when they are at their highest potency level.
Chop the harvested urtica dioica into small pieces to release their active ingredients and increase surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped urtica dioica with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka such as everclear in a clean glass container.
Steep the urtica dioica and vodka mixture in a cool dark place for approximately 2 to 3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin. The tincture's natural ingredients, such as aloe vera gel and essential oils, work together to calm the scalp and reduce itching caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing effects help to hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and flaking that can lead to itchiness. As a result, aloe tinctures provide fast and effective relief from itchy scalps, promoting a healthy and comfortable hair growth environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to itching and irritation.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in aloe vera tinctures, like salicylic acid and lactic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms that can cause itchy scalps.
Sothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects help to calm dry, irritated skin on the scalp, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce itching and irritation on the scalp.

Alkaloids (specifically aloe-emodin): Aloe-emodin has been found to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help prevent infections and reduce inflammation on the scalp, thus alleviating itchiness.

Polysaccharides (specifically acemannan): Acemannan has been found to have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce redness, itching, and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe itchy scalps.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a compound called acemannan, which is known to have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce itchiness and promote healthy scalp growth.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain a high amount of vitamins and minerals, including vitamin E and folic acid, which help to nourish and moisturize the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves for tincture preparation with a length of 4 to 6 inches.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Combine the chopped aloe vera pieces with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause irritation. The tannins in tea tree oil also have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp. Additionally, tea tree's antifungal properties help to control the growth of yeast on the scalp, a common culprit behind dandruff and flakiness. By targeting these underlying causes, tea tree tinctures provide natural relief from itchiness and promote healthy, balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antiseptic Property: Helps to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, which can cause itchiness on the scalp.
Tea Tree Oil's Anti-inflammatory Property: Reduces swelling and itching caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema, providing relief from discomfort.
Tea Tree Oil's Antioxidant Property: Neutralizes free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and cell damage on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp by creating a cooling effect and numbing the area.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help combat fungal and bacterial infections that may cause scalp irritation and itchiness.

Catechin: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions, while also protecting the scalp from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of melaleuca oil, which is commonly used to treat itchy scalps due to its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Buds: The buds are another rich source of melaleuca oil and are often used to create tinctures for their potential to soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia may also be used to create tinctures, as they contain some amount of melaleuca oil and may help alleviate itchy scalp issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers with a clean pair of scissors when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Grind 1 part of dried melaleuca alternifolia material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 part of ground melaleuca alternifolia powder in 2 parts of 80% ethanol at a ratio of 1:2 for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the tincture from the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its unique blend of essential oils, which work synergistically to calm and soothe irritated skin. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of the tincture help to reduce inflammation and prevent the overgrowth of yeast on the scalp, a common cause of itchiness. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in Tasmanian blue gum help to reduce redness and itching, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to itchy scalps caused by infections or irritations.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with scalp conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema.

Soothing and calming properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains flavonoids and terpenes that have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from itchiness, irritation, and discomfort while promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as Eucalyptol): It helps with itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and providing anti-itch relief due to its analgesic and antiseptic properties.
Catechin: It helps with itchy scalp by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itchiness.
α-Pinene: It helps with itchy scalp by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark is used for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itchiness, often attributed to their eucalyptol content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers at peak potency by cutting them early in the morning when dew is present.
Dry the eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator on 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Combine 1 part eucalyptus globulus leaves and flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, rosemary's natural oils soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching caused by conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. Additionally, rosemary's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to control the growth of yeast and bacteria that can contribute to scalp irritation. By addressing these underlying causes, rosemary tinctures provide effective relief from itchy scalp issues, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary tincture helps combat fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can cause itchiness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tincture's antioxidant activity helps neutralize free radicals that can damage scalp tissues and contribute to itchiness and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing inflammation and soothing the scalp to alleviate itchiness.
Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm the scalp and reduce itching caused by inflammation or irritation.
Bornyl acetate: A monoterpenoid that has antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to control fungal infections that can cause an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation to alleviate discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce scalp irritation.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, which can help to calm and soothe an itchy scalp, while also promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems in the morning when the plant is at its highest oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested rosmarinus officinalis material thoroughly to prevent contamination.
Combine 250 grams of the dried rosmarinus officinalis with 750 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tomato tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of their natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in tomato, such as lycopene and beta-carotene, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with an itchy scalp. Additionally, the astringent and antiseptic qualities of tomato can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness. As a result, using herbal tomato tinctures as a natural remedy can provide relief from the discomfort and irritation caused by an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tomato's antioxidant content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tomato, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to soothe and calm an itchy scalp by reducing redness and swelling.
Antimicrobial properties: Tomato's antimicrobial agents, including vitamin C and lycopene, can help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that may be causing itchiness and flakiness on the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and calm irritated skin on the scalp, reducing the sensation of itchiness.

Phenolic glycosides: These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections that may be contributing to an itchy scalp.
Saponins: These compounds have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin on the scalp from irritation and infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their antipruritic properties, which help to relieve itchiness and inflammation on the scalp.
Fruits: The ripe or unripe fruit of the tomato plant is used in tinctures due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Tomato seeds are used for their potential to reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin conditions on the scalp, as well as for their antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ripe solanum lycopersicum fruits and clean them thoroughly to prevent contamination.
Chop the solanum lycopersicum fruits into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part of the chopped solanum lycopersicum with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks while shaking the container daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The herb's essential oils, particularly eugenol and linalool, work together to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. By applying basil tincture directly to the scalp, you can calm the itchiness caused by dandruff, psoriasis, or other conditions. Additionally, basil's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections that can contribute to itchy scalps, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Basil tincture's antibacterial and antifungal properties help combat infections caused by fungal overgrowth, such as ringworm and other dermatophytes, which can lead to itching and irritation on the scalp.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and allergic reactions on the scalp, providing relief from itchy scalp symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Basil tincture's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to itchiness, dryness, and irritation on the scalp, promoting overall scalp health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the scalp, providing relief from itchiness.
Eugenol: A phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm the scalp and alleviate itchiness.
Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and soothe itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ocimum basilicum leaves are commonly used in tinctures due to their high antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers of Ocimum basilicum are used in tinctures to take advantage of their anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which help reduce itching and inflammation on the scalp.
Stems: The stems of Ocimum basilicum are used in tinctures due to their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to dry out the scalp and reduce itching and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers by cutting them at the base of the plant.
Clean the ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers by gently washing them with cold water to remove any dirt.
Combine the clean ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle to collect the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritation, providing relief from itchy scalps caused by conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. Additionally, turmeric's antifungal and antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that can contribute to scalp itchiness. By reducing inflammation and combating fungal and bacterial overgrowth, turmeric tinctures provide a holistic approach to addressing itchy scalps and promoting healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's curcuminoids reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can alleviate itching and discomfort caused by conditions like psoriasis, eczema, and seborrheic dermatitis.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage the scalp and lead to itchiness, promoting a healthier scalp environment and reducing inflammation.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Turmeric's bioactive compounds exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, which help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalps, such as ringworm and dandruff.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp conditions.
Tumerones: Tumerones are sesquiterpene compounds found in turmeric, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to calm and disinfect the scalp and reduce itchiness.
Ar-turmerone: This sesquiterpene compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp conditions, promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds like curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itchy scalps.
Roots: Roots are also widely used as they contain a significant amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness.

(There are only two parts, but I've included the third most commonly used part for information purposes) Stems: Stems, although less commonly used, may contain some bioactive compounds, but their effectiveness is generally lower compared to rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh curcuma longa roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Fill a glass jar with 1000 milliliters of 40% ethanol and add the root pieces.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and soothing properties. The tincture's ability to calm irritation and reduce redness provides instant relief from itching and scratching. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. By incorporating maidenhair tree tinctures into your hair care routine, you can say goodbye to itchy scalps for good!
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in Maidenhair Tree tincture can help to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which are often responsible for causing itchiness.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with scalp conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.
Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidant properties of Maidenhair Tree tincture can help to protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and flaking.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce irritation and inflammation on the scalp, thereby alleviating itchiness.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help control scalp infections and irritation that may cause itchiness.
Bilobalide: This triterpene is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and promoting healthy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the scalp.
Seeds: The seeds are also widely used for their ginkgolic acids, which have been reported to have antipruritic (anti-itch) properties.
Leaves: Some herbal remedies also use the leaves due to their ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the vodka for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator to slow down the extraction process for up to 6 months.
Shake the jar thoroughly before use and take 20 to 30 drops of the tincture 2 to 3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compound berberine in goldenseal has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from itchy scalps caused by conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to itching and flaking. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, herbal goldenseal tinctures effectively calm an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal's berberine content has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which helps to reduce redness and irritation associated with an itchy scalp.
Berbamine: Berbamine in goldenseal has analgesic and antipruritic effects, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by scalp irritations.
Saponins: The saponin content in goldenseal helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin health, which can alleviate itchiness and soothe the scalp.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Berberine, a primary alkaloid in Hydrastis canadensis, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and combat scalp infections that cause itchiness.

Triterpenoids: The presence of triterpenoids, specifically beta-amyrin and oleanolic acid, in Hydrastis canadensis tinctures may help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and irritation associated with conditions like dandruff or eczema.

Phenolics: The phenolic compounds in Hydrastis canadensis, including vanillic acid and protocatechuic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help alleviate itchiness and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves contain similar compounds as the roots, such as berberine, which may help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with scalp conditions.

Rhyzomes: Although less commonly mentioned than roots, rhyzomes may also contain berberine and other compounds that could contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh or 1-2 pounds of dried roots in late summer or early fall.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks or 1-2 months.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture and label it with the date and ingredients used and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. The natural antiseptic and antibacterial qualities of dandelion also help to calm fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to itchiness. Additionally, the gentle astringent properties of the herb help to balance the scalp's pH level, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dryness or oil buildup. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief for itchy scalps, promoting healthy and happy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, can reduce redness and swelling associated with itchiness, providing relief from uncomfortable itching.

Antimicrobial: The tincture's antimicrobial properties, which include triterpenoids and phenolic acids, help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff or ringworm.

Immune-stimulating: Dandelion's immunomodulatory effects, driven by its flavonoid and saponin content, can boost the body's natural defenses against external allergens and irritants, reducing inflammation and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief from itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots or leaves and clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and measure out 500 grams for each 100ml of tincture desired.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins present in elderberries have astringent properties that help to reduce redness and itching by constricting the blood vessels and reducing inflammation. Additionally, elder tincture's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections such as malassezia, a common cause of itchy scalp conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine: Elder tincture's antihistamine properties help alleviate itching and inflammation associated with skin irritations on the scalp by blocking histamine receptors and reducing allergic reactions.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of elder tincture prevent bacterial, fungal, and viral infections that can cause itchiness and irritation on the scalp, promoting healthy skin and hair growth.

Anti-inflammatory: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin irritations or allergic reactions on the scalp, providing relief and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated scalps.
Barks: Contains natural antiseptics and astringents that help reduce itching and inflammation caused by fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in the morning or evening when dewy.
Cleanse the plant material by rinsing it with cold water and gently patting dry with paper towels.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar to about 2/3 capacity.
Add 80 proof vodka as a solvent to the jar, covering the plant material completely at a ratio of 1:2 plant material:vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily, before straining and bottling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe irritated skin and calm the itch. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation and redness also helps to alleviate scalp irritation caused by dandruff, psoriasis, and other conditions. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can help to regulate the scalp's natural pH balance, reducing itching and flaking associated with an imbalanced skin environment. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Antipruritic properties: The antipruritic (itch-relieving) agents in chamomile tincture calm the itchiness, providing relief from scratching and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help soothe and protect the skin on the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Roots: Contain soothing saponins that reduce inflammation and itching, making them effective for treating scalp irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers, ensuring they are free of pesticides and contaminants.
Clean the harvested flowers by gently rinsing with filtered water to remove any debris or impurities.
Combine 1 cup of clean flowers with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing for at least 20% flower-to-liquid ratio.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid tincture.

Soothing and calming: Pot marigold's gentle, non-irritating nature allows it to soothe and calm an irritated scalp, reducing itchiness and discomfort while also helping to regulate sebum production and promote a healthy balance of scalp flora.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe itching and reduce redness.
Seeds: The seeds contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have natural antipruritic effects, providing relief from itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers from your garden or local nursery at peak bloom.
Dry the fresh calendula flowers in a warm dry place for 7-10 days.
Grind dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix one part powdered calendula with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar and let sit for six weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove sediment and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When applied topically, it calms irritated skin and reduces redness, alleviating itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, wormwood's natural antibacterial properties help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to scalp issues. As a result, the tincture soothes and balances the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and relieving the itching and irritation associated with conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Antiparasitic properties: Wormwood tincture can help kill parasitic mites and lice that may be causing itchiness on the scalp, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as absinthin and aristolochic acid, can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms that may contribute to scalp conditions, including fungal infections like ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Barks: The barks contain thujone, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce itching and inflammation associated with an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems for the highest quality extraction.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent penetration.
Combine 1 part chopped plant material with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The oil extracted from lemongrass has been shown to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing itching and flaking caused by conditions like dandruff and psoriasis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of lemongrass help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness and irritation. As a result, using herbal lemongrass tinctures can provide relief from itchy scalp issues and promote a healthy, balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass tincture's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itchiness caused by fungal or bacterial infections.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in lemongrass tincture effectively combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and seborrhoeic dermatitis.

Analgesic and anti-pruritic properties: Lemongrass tincture's analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-pruritic (itch-relieving) properties provide quick relief from itchy scalp sensations, allowing for a more comfortable scalp and reduced scratching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing itchiness and flaking.

Rhyzomes: As the main storage organ of the plant, lemongrass rhyzomes contain high concentrations of essential oils and bioactive compounds, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures to treat scalp issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cymbopogon citratus leaves in abundance for at least two ounces of dried material.
Dry the fresh leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 1-2 hours.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine with high-proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to four parts vodka.
Store the tincture mixture in a clean glass jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the bottle with date and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause scalp irritation. The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme also reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy scalps. Additionally, thyme's antiparasitic effects help to eliminate lice and other parasites that can lead to itching. By soothing and calming the scalp, herbal thyme tinctures provide quick relief from itchy scalp symptoms, promoting a healthy and comfortable scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tincture's antimicrobial properties help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by fungal, bacterial, or yeast infections on the scalp.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Thyme's natural compounds, such as thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial organisms that can cause itchy scalp conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema.
Anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tincture's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing and calming an itchy scalp.
Buds: The buds of the thyme plant contain high concentrations of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems, collecting a minimum of 2 ounces for the recipe.
Combine the harvested thymus vulgaris material with high-proof vodka in a glass jar, using a ratio of one part herbs to three parts solvent.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Label and store the final herbal thymus vulgaris tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, ready for use at a dosage of 20-30 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The natural oils in fennel tincture penetrate deep into the scalp, reducing inflammation and itching associated with conditions like psoriasis, eczema, and dandruff. Additionally, fennel's astringent properties help to balance the scalp's natural pH, further reducing itchiness and promoting healthy hair growth. Regular use of herbal fennel tinctures can bring relief from itchy scalps and promote overall scalp health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial agents combat fungal, bacterial, and parasitic infections that can cause itchiness, flakiness, and dandruff on the scalp.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel tincture's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to an itchy, irritated scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural antihistamines that can reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions like psoriasis and eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is present for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:4.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily, before straining.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 2 years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy scalp are a natural and effective way to soothe and calm an irritated scalp. These concentrated liquid extracts, made from botanicals such as tea tree oil, lavender, and chamomile, can be applied directly to the scalp to reduce inflammation and itchiness. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with itchy scalp include peppermint, which cools and calms the skin, and lemongrass, which has anti-inflammatory properties. By using these tinctures, individuals can experience a significant reduction in itching and discomfort, allowing them to feel more confident and comfortable in their own skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, acne, and psoriasis. These conditions can cause dryness, redness, and irritation on the skin, leading to discomfort and itching. Herbal tinctures such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm these conditions, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with itchy scalp is a blend of Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia), Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia), and Calendula (Calendula officinalis). Tea Tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections, while Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the scalp. Calendula oil's antioxidant properties promote skin regeneration and reduce irritation, making this combination a powerful natural remedy to alleviate itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Chinese medicine, herbal tinctures can be effective in treating itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects. The study analyzed a compound traditional Chinese medicine dermatitis ointment (CTCMDO) composed of five plant extracts, which was found to suppress DNCB-induced scratching behavior in mice in a dose-dependent manner. The active ingredients of CTCMDO were identified through high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) and liquid chromatography/mass spectrometer (LC/MS) analysis, and it was determined that CTCMDO effectively improved atopic dermatitis (AD) by inhibiting the production of Th1/2 cytokines and suppressing itch-related mediators, suggesting that herbal tinctures may be beneficial for itchy scalp conditions.
